evidence: total R&D expenses of $3.1 billion for both Fiscal 2026 and Fiscal 2025 and $2.8 billion for Fiscal 2024
we have increased our purchases of certain components with suppliers, which has resulted in increased inventory levels, higher purchase obligations, and new working capital dynamics
we held strategic investments in non-marketable securities of $1.6 billion and $1.5 billion as of January 30, 2026 and January 31, 2025
we disaggregated our servers and networking offerings within revenue by major product category into AI-optimized servers offerings and traditional servers and networking offerings
AI-optimized servers designed to handle the most demanding compute-intensive workloads, including AI model training, fine-tuning, and inferencing
increase in ISG net revenue was primarily driven by growth in our AI-optimized servers offerings... net revenue increased by 19%
use of AI technologies, including generative and agentic AI, may make us susceptible to unanticipated security threats from adversaries as we incorporate such technologies into our internal systems

AI solutions have been purchased primarily by a small number of larger customers and cloud service providers... large orders may cause fluctuations in our results of operations... intense competition and pricing pressure

laws, rules, directives and regulations governing the use of AI are rapidly proliferating and evolving... failure or perceived failure to comply could impair our ability to claim intellectual property ownership

limitations: R&D figures of $3.1B are total company R&D, not AI-specific; no dedicated AI capex dollar amount is disclosed, limiting investment intensity score to 3 rather than 4
AI-optimized server revenue growth is described qualitatively and as a driver of 19% ISG revenue increase, but no standalone AI revenue figure is broken out in these excerpts
Risk disclosure is detailed and company-specific (score 4) but no quantified risk exposure or named governance oversight body is cited in the excerpts, precluding score 5
Excerpts do not include a dedicated capital expenditure table; any AI-specific capex line items may exist elsewhere in the filing but are not visible here
